title: zea mays subsp. amylacea
intro: Zea mays subsp. amylacea, also known as waxy maize or high amylose corn, is a subspecies of corn that is characterized by its high amylose content, which makes it a valuable source of dietary fiber and resistant starch. The medicinal properties of Zea mays subsp. amylacea include its ability to lower cholesterol levels and regulate blood sugar levels, making it a potential treatment for conditions such as hyperlipidemia and type 2 diabetes. The health benefits of consuming Zea mays subsp. amylacea include improved digestion, weight management, and reduced risk of cardiovascular disease. Zea mays subsp. amylacea can be prepared in various herbal forms, including capsules, tablets, and powdered extracts, which can be used to support digestive health and manage blood sugar levels.
